The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to completion tier 5]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:mass bump]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Default sort converted to citation needed]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]]

The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940年}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940年}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:New]]

The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year }} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:New]]

{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940th year }} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year }} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year }} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category: tier 4]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:New]]

However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Chinjusha]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:History of Kyoto]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:Kurama]] {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} [[Category:Completion tier 4]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]]{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}{{citation needed|Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]][[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

Bot: 記事改名に伴う内部リンク修正 (ケーブルカー関連) ([[User:YushuBot/T25111|info]])
revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=由岐神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | 所在地=[[京都府]][[京都市]][[左京区]][[鞍馬]]本町1073 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=由岐神社}} | 祭神=[[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] | 創建=[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]） | 別名=靫明神 | 例祭=[[10月22日]]（[[鞍馬の火祭]]） | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=由岐神社|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''由岐神社'''（ゆきじんじゃ）は、[[京都市]][[左京区]][[鞍馬]]本町にある[[神社]]。通称'''靫明神'''（ゆきみょうじん）。[[大国主|大己貴命]]と[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]]を主祭神として「'''由岐大明神'''」と総称し、八所大明神を相殿に祀る。[[鞍馬寺]]の[[鎮守社]]である。 == 歴史 == 祭神は元は宮中に祀られていたが、[[平安京]]で大[[地震]]が起き、[[天慶の乱]]まで発生したので当時の[[天皇]]である[[朱雀天皇]]が天下泰平と万民幸福を祈念し、その勅によって[[天慶]]3年（[[940年]]）、[[鞍馬山]]の地に遷宮をして[[鞍馬寺]]の[[鎮守社]]とし、都の北方鎮護を仰せつけた。例祭の[[鞍馬の火祭]]は、そのときに里人がかがり火を持って神霊を迎えたことによるものである。 「靫明神」という社名は、天皇の病や国難時に神前に[[靫]]（ゆき）を献じて平穏を祈ったことによる。洛中の[[五條天神社 (京都市)|五條天神社]]では、国難時にその責任を取って「流罪に処す」として国の役人が神社の扉に靫を架けて閉じるということが行われていたが、『[[徒然草]]』によれば由岐神社でも同様のことが行われていたという。由岐神社と五條天神社は祭神が同じである。 [[慶長]]12年（[[1607年]]）には[[豊臣秀頼]]によって本殿及び拝殿が再建されている。 == 祭神 == * 主祭神 - [[大国主|大己貴命]]、[[スクナビコナ|少彦名命]] * 相殿 - 八所大明神 == 境内 == * 本殿 - 2023年に改築。 * 三宝荒神社 - 祭神：[[三宝荒神|三宝荒神大神]]。火の神、竈の神として、古くからこの地に祀られている。信仰：火難除けの神。 * 社務所 * 白長弁財天社 - 祭神：[[弁才天|弁財天]]。 * 冠者社 - 祭神：[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]。信仰：商売繁昌の神様。 * 岩上社 - 祭神：[[事代主|事代主命]]・[[オオヤマツミ|大山祇命]]。 * 大杉社 - 祭神：御神木。信仰：願掛け杉の御神木。大杉さん。 * [[八幡宮]]社 - 祭神：[[八幡神|八幡大神]]。 * 拝殿（割拝殿、[[重要文化財]]） - 慶長12年（1607年）に豊臣秀頼により再建。[[神社建築#拝殿の建築様式|割拝殿]]形式の[[安土桃山時代|桃山]]建築。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財 === * 拝殿（割拝殿） * 石造狛犬 一対{{Cite web|和書|url= http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html |title=由岐神社とは|publisher=由岐神社 |accessdate=2019-10-06 }} - [[京都国立博物館]]寄託。同博物館では「石造獅子」として展示している{{Cite web|和書|url= https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html |title=特集陳列獅子と狛犬 |publisher=京都国立博物館 |accessdate=2019-10-06 }}。 === 京都市指定天然記念物 === * 大杉社の御神木 - [[スギ|杉]]3本、[[カゴノキ]]1本 === 京都市登録無形民俗文化財 === * [[鞍馬火祭]] == その他 == * 神社には拝観料などはなく神社境内は無料で入場できるが、鞍馬寺の境内にあるために、鞍馬寺の入山料である「愛山料」が高校生以上500円（中学生以下は無料・[[叡山電鉄|叡電]]1日乗車券提示者は優待・早朝と夜間は無料）必要である。 * 鞍馬寺山門の門内すぐの所から出ている[[鞍馬寺鞍馬山鋼索鉄道|鞍馬山鋼索鉄道]]に乗車すると、由岐神社を通りすぎてしまう。そのため先に鞍馬寺の本堂である本殿金堂などに参拝したあと、由岐神社に参拝するならば下りは徒歩で参道を下ることが必要である。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[豊臣秀頼]] * [[安土桃山時代]] * [[神社建築]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} *[http://www.yukijinjya.jp 由岐神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:鞍馬]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]]

[[スギ|杉]]3本、[[カゴノキ]]1本 === 京都市登録無形民俗文化財 === * [[鞍馬火祭]] == その他 == * 神社には拝観料などはなく神社境内は無料で入場できるが、鞍馬寺の境内にあるために、鞍馬寺の入山料である「愛山料」が高校生以上500円（中学生以下は無料・[[叡山電鉄|叡電]]1日乗車券提示者は優待・早朝と夜間は無料）必要である。 * 鞍馬寺山門の門内すぐの所から出ている[[鞍馬寺鞍馬山鋼索鉄道|鞍馬山鋼索鉄道]]に乗車すると、由岐神社を通りすぎてしまう。そのため先に鞍馬寺の本堂である本殿金堂などに参拝したあと、由岐神社に参拝するならば下りは徒歩で参道を下ることが必要である。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * [[豊臣秀頼]] * [[安土桃山時代]] * [[神社建築]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} *[http://www.yukijinjya.jp 由岐神社]（公式サイト） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ゆきしんしや}} [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:鎮守社]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:鞍馬]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * {{ill|Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]
