However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyō-ku|qq=}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/940|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 22|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyō-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chinjusha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama-dera|qq=}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian-kyō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurama|qq=}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/940|qq=}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurezuregusa|qq=}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1607|qq=}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto National Museum|qq=}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Momoyama-period architecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto National Museum|qq=}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyō-ku|qq=}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} | founded = {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/940|qq=}} ({{ill|Tenkei|ja|天慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenkei|qq=}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|ja|10月22日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 22|qq=}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku|ja|左京区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakyō-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chinjusha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama-dera|qq=}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian-kyō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurama|qq=}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/940|qq=}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurezuregusa|qq=}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1607|qq=}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto National Museum|qq=}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

The shrine serves as the {{ill|chinjusha|ja|鎮守社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/chinjusha|qq=}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|ja|鞍馬寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama-dera|qq=}}. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian-kyō|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|ja|鞍馬山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Kurama|qq=}} in {{ill|940|ja|940th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/940|qq=}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurezuregusa|qq=}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1607|qq=}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto National Museum|qq=}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|ja|徒然草|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsurezuregusa|qq=}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|ja|1607年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1607|qq=}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman shrine|lt=Hachiman Shrine|ja|八幡宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|ja|京都国立博物館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto National Museum|qq=}}, displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} * {{ill|Momoyama Period|ja|安土桃山時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Momoyama Period|qq=}} * {{ill|Shinto Architecture|ja|神社建築|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Architecture|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]]

It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]]

The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]]

Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]]. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]

The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:左京区の建築物]] [[Category:左京区の歴史]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:鞍馬]]

According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Buildings in Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[en:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kurama, Sakyō-ku]]

The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Kurama]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Kurama, Sakyō-ku]] {{Commons category|Yuki-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]] Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = [[:en:940|940]] ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = [[:en:October 22|October 22]] ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]]. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:京都市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Yuki-jinja}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "の重要文化財" to "の Important Cultural Properties"

Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Yuki-jinja}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg|250px]]
Worship hall {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=250|coord={{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]] Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = [[:en:940|940]] ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = [[:en:October 22|October 22]] ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} }} '''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]]. == History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|qq=}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation. The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities. The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori. == Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami == Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|qq=}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607). == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}. === Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus) === Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}} == Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach. == References == {{Reflist}} == Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]] == External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine] {{Shinto Sidebar}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{translated page|ja|由岐神社|version=104641063|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Chinjusha]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:History of Kyoto]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[ja:%E7%94%B1%E5%B2%90%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:Yuki Shrine (Kyoto)]] [[ja:由岐神社]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Kyoto City]] [[Category:Buildings in Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Sakyo-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Edo period architecture]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Yuki-jinja}} [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]
