<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577577]] Wikidata name: Yuki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]] Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = [[:en:940|940]] ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = [[:en:October 22|October 22]] ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]].
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

== Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]]

== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577577]] Wikidata name: Yuki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]] Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = [[:en:940|940]] ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = [[:en:October 22|October 22]] ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]].
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11577577]] Wikidata name: Yuki Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]] [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]] [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]] Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = [[:en:940|940]] ([[:en:Tengyō|Tenkei]] 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = [[:en:October 22|October 22]] ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|Sakyō-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]]. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the [[:en:Chinjusha|chinjusha]] of [[:en:Kurama-dera|Kurama-dera]].
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]] and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to [[:en:Mount Kurama|Mount Kurama]] in [[:en:940|940]] (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to [[:en:Tsurezuregusa|Tsurezuregusa]], this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in [[:en:1607|1607]] (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * [[:en:Hachiman shrine|Hachiman Shrine]] * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the [[:en:Kyoto National Museum|Kyoto National Museum]], displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the [[:en:Kurama-dera_Cable|Kurama-dera Cable]] pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] * [[:en:Azuchi–Momoyama period|Momoyama Period]] * [[:en:Shinto architecture|Shinto Architecture]]
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine]

'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera}}.

Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.

The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

== Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori}} * {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama Period}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto Architecture}}

'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera}}.
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera}}.

== History ==
Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.

The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.

The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.

== Enshrined Deities ==
* Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}
* Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami

== Grounds ==
* Main Hall - Renovated in 2023.
* Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection.
* Shrine Office
* Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}.
* Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity.
* Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}.
* Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}.
* {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}}
* Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Properties ===
* Worship Hall (割拝殿)
* Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.

=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments ===
* Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)

=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties ===
* {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}

== Additional Information ==
* Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free.
* Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

== Related Articles ==

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yuki-jinja]][[ja:由岐神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera}}.
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori}} * {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama Period}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto Architecture}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine]

The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera}}.
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Tengyō Rebellion|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbō Kōjin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori}} * {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama Period}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto Architecture}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940|1=940}} ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22|1=October 22}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504|1=Three Medicine Gods}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224|1=kakezukuri}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera|1=Kurama-dera}}.
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Q11442602|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama|1=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940|1=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=Yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa|1=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607|1=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum|1=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable|1=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.

The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607|1=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum|1=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable|1=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Yuki Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Yuki-jinja 20100605.jpg | location = {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}} {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} Kurama Honmachi 1073 | position = {{coord|35|06|57.9|N|135|46|17.1|E|region:JP-26_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Yuki Shrine}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940|1=940}} ({{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|WD=Q1327340|lt=Tenkei|1=Tengyō}} 3) | other names = Yuki Myōjin | festival = {{ill|October 22|en|October 22|WD=Q2957|lt=October 22|1=October 22}} ({{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}}) | map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto Prefecture|label=Yuki Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|relief=1|width=210|caption=}} | location = {{ill|Sakyō-ku|WD=Q1321832|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.116083|N|135.771417|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Q11622504|WD=Q11622504|1=Three Medicine Gods}} | architecture_style = {{ill|kakezukuri|WD=Q94062224|1=kakezukuri}} }}
'''Yuki Shrine''' (由岐神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Sakyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1321832|lt=Sakyō-ku|1=Sakyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}. Also referred to as '''Yuki Myōjin''' (靫明神), it enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} and {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} as its main deities, collectively called "Yuki Daimyōjin." The shrine serves as the {{ill|Chinjusha|en|Chinjusha|WD=Q13479654|lt=chinjusha|1=Chinjusha}} of {{ill|Kurama-dera|en|Kurama-dera|WD=Q608668|lt=Kurama-dera|1=Kurama-dera}}.
== History == Originally, the enshrined deities were worshipped within the imperial palace. Following a series of disasters, including a major earthquake in {{ill|Heian-kyō|en|Heian-kyō|WD=Q751907|lt=Heian-kyō|1=Heian-kyō}} and the {{ill|Tengyō Rebellion|ja|天慶の乱|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tengyō Rebellion|1=Q11442602|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442602}}, Emperor Suzaku ordered the relocation of the shrine to {{ill|Mount Kurama|en|Mount Kurama|WD=Q410132|lt=Mount Kurama|1=Mount Kurama}} in {{ill|940|en|940|WD=Q23830|lt=940|1=940}} (Tenkei 3). It was established as the chinju shrine of Kurama-dera to protect the northern border of the capital. The {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬の火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|qq=}} originates from the villagers' torchlight procession to welcome the shrine's deities during this relocation.
The name "Yuki Myōjin" derives from the practice of offering {{ill|quiver|ja|靫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/quiver|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 箙|1=Yebira|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17061580}} (yuki) to the shrine during times of national crisis, praying for peace and stability. According to {{ill|Tsurezuregusa|en|Tsurezuregusa|WD=Q1350535|lt=Tsurezuregusa|1=Tsurezuregusa}}, this tradition was similar to practices at {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine|ja|五條天神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine|qq=}}, which enshrines the same deities.
The worship hall and main hall were rebuilt in {{ill|1607|en|1607|WD=Q6756|lt=1607|1=1607}} (Keichō 12) under the patronage of Toyotomi Hideyori.
== Enshrined Deities == * Main deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} * Auxiliary deities: Eight additional kami
== Grounds == * Main Hall - Renovated in 2023. * Sanpōkōjin Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kōjin|ja|三宝荒神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Sanbo Kojin|WD=Q13577821}}, revered as a deity of fire and hearth protection. * Shrine Office * Shironaga Benzaiten Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Benzaiten|ja|弁財天|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Benzaiten|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 弁才天}}. * Kanja Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素戔嗚命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, a deity of prosperity. * Iwakami Shrine - Dedicated to {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} and {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}}. * Ōsugi Shrine - Features sacred trees, including three cedar trees and one {{ill|Machilus|ja|カゴノキ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Machilus|1=Litsea coreana|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11092773}}. * {{ill|Hachiman shrine|en|Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine}} * Worship Hall (割拝殿) - Designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in the Momoyama architectural style, rebuilt in Keichō 12 (1607).
== Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties === * Worship Hall (割拝殿) * Pair of stone komainu - Deposited at the {{ill|Kyoto National Museum|en|Kyoto National Museum|WD=Q147286|lt=Kyoto National Museum|1=Kyoto National Museum}}, displayed as "stone lions"<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.yukijinjya.jp/about/index.html|title=About Yuki Shrine|publisher=Yuki Shrine|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.kyohaku.go.jp/jp/theme/floor1_5/f1_5_koremade/shishi_2016.html|title=Special Exhibit: Lions and Komainu|publisher=Kyoto National Museum|accessdate=October 6, 2019}}</ref>.
=== Kyoto City Natural Monuments === * Sacred trees of Ōsugi Shrine (three cedar trees and one machilus)
=== Kyoto City Intangible Folk Cultural Properties === * {{ill|Kurama Fire Festival|ja|鞍馬火祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurama Fire Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 鞍馬の火祭}}
== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable|1=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.

== Additional Information == * Entry to the shrine grounds is free. However, since it is within the precincts of Kurama-dera, visitors must pay the temple's admission fee of 500 yen for high school students and older (free for younger children and discounts for Eizan Railway one-day pass holders). Early morning and evening access is free. * Visitors using the {{ill|Kurama-dera_Cable|en|Kurama-dera_Cable|WD=Q6445551|lt=Kurama-dera Cable|1=Kurama-dera Cable}} pass by Yuki Shrine. To visit after worshiping at the Kurama-dera Main Hall, visitors must walk back down the approach.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|en|Toyotomi Hideyori|WD=Q313641|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}} * {{ill|Azuchi–Momoyama period|en|Azuchi–Momoyama period|WD=Q319531|lt=Momoyama Period|1=Azuchi–Momoyama period}} * {{ill|Shinto architecture|en|Shinto architecture|WD=Q3080012|lt=Shinto Architecture|1=Shinto architecture}}
== External Links == {{Commonscat|Yuki-jinja}} * [http://www.yukijinjya.jp Official Website of Yuki Shrine]
